excerpt: In the terminology of interpretation, the good deeds refer to the Imams (a.s.), because they are the ones who invite them to Paradise. In the same way, the evil deeds are in fact the Imams of al-Dhalal, because they are misleading.
source_location_hint: Esoteric interpretation of hadith concerning love of Imam

excerpt: The Messenger of Allah said: 'O Ali! Your friend is my friend, and your enemy is my enemy.'
source_work: Musnad Ahmad; transmitted in Ismaili sources
source_location_hint: Hadith regarding the Prophet's declaration of ʿAlī's status

excerpt: Whoever recites it sincerely enters Paradise. When asked what is this sincerity, he said: Getting to know the spiritual and physical limits that this word signifies and fulfilling their rights.
source_work: Transmitted in Ismaili exegetical sources; parallel to al-Kirmānī's Rahat al-ʿAql
source_location_hint: Definition of sincere declaration and its conditions

excerpt: Amīr al-Muʾminīn is the first and the last in this sense: the beginning of knowledge of the Word of sincere faith originates from him, understanding it depends upon him, and ultimate salvation is likewise bound to his essence.
source_location_hint: Exposition of ʿAlī's role in knowledge of tawḥīd

excerpt: The manifest and the hidden are not attributes of the Creator, but attributes of His creation, which He has fashioned by His power and held within His dominion.
source_location_hint: Theological distinction between God's nature and creation's attributes

excerpt: The believer is unknown in this world but well known in the highest assembly.
source_location_hint: Description of the believer's station in this world and the next

excerpt: the intention of the believer proves more beneficial than the action itself
the believer acquires righteous knowledge from authentic teachers, which brings peace and tranquility to heart and mind
source_location_hint: Commentary on hadith of the bee

excerpt: the Imam stands with both arms of the Shariah, embracing outward law and inward wisdom, exhorting followers to remain attached to both
source_authority: Ismaili interpretation of scales of judgment
source_location_hint: Commentary on good character in the scales

excerpt: connection with the Imam constitutes genuine prayer; sincere service during occultation brings beauty in the period of unveiling
source_location_hint: Commentary on night prayer and facial beauty

excerpt: the daʿwa al-hādiyyah constitutes the paradise of truth, and Lahūt al-Imāmī is the paradise of the Imam—the true Paradise itself
the Prophet recited 'O Book of Allah, speak!' and when the Qur'an gave no answer, declared: 'I am the speaking Qur'an, and this is the silent Qur'an'
source_authority: Ismaili transmission regarding the Qur'an and ʿAlī
source_location_hint: Commentary on hadith of Qur'an as intercessor; Battle of Ṣiffīn episode

excerpt: All narratives preserved in the Qur'an serve a singular purpose: to demonstrate that events witnessed in earlier generations recur in succeeding ages, though often in altered form.

Those few who boarded the Ark of the Prophet's family were delivered from error and bound themselves to the household of the Prophet. Though small in number, these adherents constitute the true Ahl al-Sunna wa-l-Jama'a according to Qur'anic testimony.

The quintessence of all things is invariably scarce and refined. Among humankind, the wise and learned are few, whilst the ignorant and debased are many... So too are prophets and saints few among humanity, whilst the foolish and heedless are legion.

The Ahl al-Bayt, inheriting the Prophet's wisdom, perceived within each command a deeper meaning: they related every outward ordinance to its inner reality, and beheld in every parable an exemplum of esoteric truth. Action devoid of meaning is a body without soul; knowledge bereft of practice is a soul without body.

The esoteric interpretation: the enemies of the Ahl al-Bayt represent an image of Hell itself. When questioned and presented with queries to which they cannot respond, they roar and threaten. Yet when silenced by true knowledge, their voice becomes extinguished.
